title: Occam’s Razor
author: From: The Great Mental Models
content: When presented with competing hypotheses, the simplest explanation is usually the correct one.
action: Action: Strip a convoluted plan down to its simplest necessary steps.

title: Inversion
author: From: Poor Charlie’s Almanack
content: Don't just think about how to succeed; think about how to fail and then avoid those things.
action: Action: Spend 10 minutes listing exactly how to ruin your current project, then create a plan to prevent those specific actions.

title: Hanlon’s Razor
author: From: The Great Mental Models
content: Never attribute to malice that which is adequately explained by stupidity or misunderstanding.
action: Action: Replace an 'assumption of intent' with an 'assumption of misunderstanding' in a tense situation.

title: First Principles Thinking
author: From: The Art of Thinking Clearly
content: Boil things down to their fundamental truths and reason up from there, rather than reasoning by analogy.
action: Action: Strip a complex problem down to its basic, unchangeable facts.

title: Circle of Competence
author: From: Poor Charlie’s Almanack
content: You don't have to be an expert on everything. You only need to know the perimeter of your own competence.
action: Action: Define the one area where you are truly an expert.
